display.purpose: Hero headlines, property titles, section headers. Georgia serif, never sans.

body.purpose: All prose copy. Geist Sans, capped ~65-75ch line length.

label.purpose: Chapter eyebrows, badges, system metadata, status labels. Mono, uppercase, 0.18em tracking -- the system's core signature.

shadows.name: sm
shadows.purpose: Subtle lift for list items, small raised chips.

shadows.name: glow
shadows.purpose: Gold-tinted -- active/focused accent elements only, a signal not decoration.

shadows.name: glow-soft
shadows.purpose: Softer gold glow for ambient accent emphasis.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The system's one loud interactive element -- Spatial Gold Bright fill, void-black text.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #F7C64E; color: #0e0e0e; font-family: var(--font-geist-sans), sans-serif; font-weight: 600; padding: 10px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 4px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 0.25s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #E8AE3C; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 1.5px solid #E8AE3C; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action inside a panel where a primary CTA would be too loud.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { background: transparent; color: #c8c8c8; border: 1px solid #6E531A; padding: 10px 24px; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; transition: color 0.25s, border-color 0.25s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: #E8AE3C; border-color: #E8AE3C; }

components.name: Chapter Eyebrow
components.description: Signature numbered chapter marker opening every property-page section (e.g. 01 -- THE SPACE), followed by a hairline divider.
components.css: .ds-chapter-eyebrow span { font-family: var(--font-geist-mono), monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #E8AE3C; display: block; margin-bottom: 10px; } .ds-chapter-rule { height: 1px; background: #262626; }

components.name: Panel / Card
components.description: Tonal-layered surface, hairline border, no drop shadow at rest.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #161616; border: 0.5px solid #262626; border-radius: 4px; padding: 20px; color: #f0ede8; }

components.name: Floating Glass Panel
components.description: Scoped glassmorphism for genuinely floating chrome (toolbox, modals) -- never static content cards.
components.css: .ds-glass-panel { background: rgba(17,17,17,0.85); backdrop-filter: blur(12px); -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(12px); border: 1px solid rgba(232,174,60,0.2); border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 12px 48px rgba(0,0,0,0.75); }

components.name: Nav Pill (active)
components.description: Active navigation state -- gold + soft glow, mono uppercase label.
components.css: .ds-nav-pill { font-family: var(--font-geist-mono), monospace; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #c8c8c8; text-decoration: none; padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 20px; transition: color 0.25s, background 0.25s; } .ds-nav-pill-active { color: #F7C64E; background: rgba(232,174,60,0.08); box-shadow: 0 0 18px rgba(232,174,60,0.18); }

narrative.overview: ScoutIt isn't a real estate listings board wearing a dark theme -- it's an intelligence terminal for Philippine space, built for people deciding whether to trust it enough to act. The system runs on restraint: 95% void-black canvas, 5% Spatial Gold, and every use of that gold has to earn its place. Chrome stays quiet so the actual signal -- flood risk, verification badges, market data, category intelligence -- can carry the visual weight instead of decoration doing it. The name is deliberate: this treats real estate, short-term rentals, hospitality, restaurants, and event venues as one category -- "spatial commerce" -- not six separate templates bolted together.
keyCharacteristics: Void-black canvas with tonal surface layering, not pure flat black
Gold is a signal, not wallpaper -- deployed with intent, never decoratively
Mono-spaced, wide-tracked, uppercase labels for system chrome
Georgia serif for display headlines -- editorial weight, not tech-startup sans-everywhere
Numbered chapter registry on property pages is real information architecture, not decoration

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Gold renders on <=5% of any given screen. If a section reads gold-heavy, it's decorating, not signaling.

rules.name: The No-Pure-Black Rule
rules.body: Never #000000 as a surface color; void-black (#0e0e0e) always carries the intended warmth.

rules.name: The Instrument-Label Rule
rules.body: Any UI chrome that isn't a headline or body copy is mono, uppercase, and wide-tracked. The system's most recognizable signature.

rules.name: The Glow-Is-a-Signal Rule
rules.body: Gold glow means interactive and currently relevant, not decorative flourish.

dos: Do keep gold to <=5% of any screen -- it's a signal, not a theme color.
Do use the mono/uppercase/wide-tracked treatment for every piece of system chrome.
Do use tonal surface-stepping plus subtle neutral shadows for depth; reserve gold glow for genuinely interactive/focused elements only.
Do treat the numbered chapter registry as real information architecture.
Do scope glassmorphism to genuinely floating chrome -- never static content.
donts: Don't read as a generic PH listings directory (Lamudi/Zillow-style board).
Don't reach for common luxury real estate cliches -- gold-and-marble old-money framing, ornate serif-everywhere, stock-photo-mansion imagery.
Don't use pure #000000 as a surface.
Don't apply gold decoratively -- every gold use should be a deliberate signal.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe.
Don't apply the numbered-chapter/eyebrow pattern reflexively outside the property-page chapter system.
